generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance VehicleSteeringActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuator controlling vehicle steering based on autonomous driving algorithms"
]

instance EngineControlActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator managing engine power output and fuel injection for performance optimization"
]

instance BatteryManagementActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controls battery charging/discharging and thermal management for EV systems"
]

instance EmergencyBrakeActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Emergency braking system triggered via remote management interface"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance LiDARPointCloudSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR providing 360° point cloud data for obstacle detection"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance DepthCameraSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel Deepsense D435i Depth Camera capturing stereo visual data for environment mapping"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Combination of embedded sensors measuring motor, steering, servo actuation, and overall system state"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

instance SensorCalibrationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "System for monitoring and adjusting sensor calibration parameters"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SensorCalibrationData
]

// C4: Data Transmissions
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARPointCloudSensor
]

instance DepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DepthCameraSensor
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance SensorCalibrationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SensorCalibrationSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance LiDARProcessingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model applying DBSCAN clustering and Kalman filtering to LiDAR point clouds for noise reduction and object tracking"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EdgeProcessingEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation model representing vehicle dynamics and state transitions for autonomous driving"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EdgeProcessingEnabler, CloudProcessingEnabler
]

instance AnomalyDetectionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Machine learning model for detecting sensor drift and hardware failures in real-time"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CloudProcessingEnabler
]

instance DataLoggingDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical database storing post-mortem analysis data for vehicle performance validation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
]

instance OperationalConstraintDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Database containing predefined operational constraints (speed limits, obstacle avoidance thresholds)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ValidationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataTranslationFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Feature Engine converting raw sensor data into structured formats via ROS2 network for compatibility"
]

instance EdgeProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson-based edge computing platform handling low-latency processing of critical driving decisions"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance CloudProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cloud-based processing infrastructure for complex simulations and AI model inference"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance DataStorageEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Database management system (Apache Kafka + MongoDB) for storing and processing data streams"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataLoggingDatabase, OperationalConstraintDatabase
]

instance ValidationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "System validating physical vehicle performance against operational constraints in real-time"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance SecurityEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cybersecurity framework implementing Zero Trust architecture with MQTT/gRPC protocols for secure communication"
]

instance ISO23247ComplianceEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Framework ensuring compliance with ISO 23247 standards for manufacturing digital twins"
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time synchronization of sensor data between physical and digital twins with ≤100ms latency"
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight, PerformanceValidationAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service offloading computationally intensive tasks (path planning, AI inference) to cloud-edge hybrid architecture"
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendation, FaultPredictionAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling remote control and diagnostics via encrypted MQTT/gRPC channels for teleoperation"
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemHealthMonitoring, EmergencyInterventionAction
]

instance LifecyclePredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service forecasting remaining operational life of components using degradation models"
    DTDFVocab:provides ComponentHealthInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time detection of sensor drift and hardware failures triggering alerts for investigation"
]

instance PerformanceValidationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Validation of physical vehicle performance against operational constraints (speed limits, obstacle avoidance)"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance OptimizationRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Context-aware recommendations for vehicle performance optimization via predictive analytics"
]

instance FaultPredictionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic triggering of maintenance procedures based on predicted component failures"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SystemHealthMonitoring : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time telemetry visualization including battery status, sensor calibration, and system diagnostics"
]

instance EmergencyInterventionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote execution of emergency braking, ACC disablement, or calibration adjustments"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ComponentHealthInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecasted remaining operational life of critical components (LiDAR modules, sensors)"
]

// C1: Environment
instance AutonomousVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains VehicleSteeringActuator, EngineControlActuator,
                BatteryManagementActuator, EmergencyBrakeActuator,
                LiDARPointCloudSensor, DepthCameraSensor,
                VehicleStateSensor, SensorCalibrationSensor,
                LiDARProcessingModel, VehicleStateSimulationModel,
                AnomalyDetectionModel, DataTranslationFE,
                EdgeProcessingEnabler, CloudProcessingEnabler,
                DataStorageEnabler, ValidationEnabler,
                SecurityEnabler, ISO23247ComplianceEnabler,
                RealTimeMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService,
                RemoteManagementService, LifecyclePredictionService
]

instance AutonomousVehiclePhysicalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "1/10th-scale autonomous vehicle physical entity with embedded sensors and actuators"
]

instance AutonomousVehicleDigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Digital representation of the physical autonomous vehicle with real-time synchronization capabilities"
]

instance AutonomousVehicleUserInterface : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "3D visualization and control panel interface for monitoring and managing the digital twin"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** involves control commands transmitted from the digital twin’s **Actuation FE** and **Controlling FE** to the physical vehicle. Key components include:
- **Actuation FE** converts desired driving parameters (e.g., speed, steering commands) into **motor voltage commands** and servo actuation signals.
- **Controlling FE** (e.g., ACC - Adaptive Cruise Control) determines vehicle following behavior by analyzing lead vehicle data and computing **desired speed adjustments** for the ego vehicle.

**Command Types & Formats**:
- Commands are generated by **SW components** (e.g., ACC Controlling FE) and transmitted via **ROS2 network** (internal) or **Wi-Fi UDP** (external).
- No specific message types (e.g., ROS topics like '/steering_cmd') or message formats (e.g., geometry_msgs/Twist) are detailed in the documents.
- **No explicit validation/authentication mechanisms** are described for command execution, but the **Data Translation FE** ensures proper serialization and unit conversion.

**Feedback Systems & Conflict Resolution**:
- The system does not explicitly mention feedback loops or conflict resolution for control commands.
- **Remote Management FE** enables configuration changes (e.g., enabling/disabling ACC, modifying desired speed) but does not specify validation rules or priority-based conflict resolution.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
    The Digital Twin system enforces **strict time synchronization** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and real-time coordination. The system mandates **sub-100 ms clock accuracy** for safety-critical vehicular applications, driven by hardware limitations and the need for precise temporal alignment across distributed entities. This requirement ensures deterministic timing for sensor-actuator loops, data processing pipelines (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking), and inter-entity communication. The **ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA)** does not explicitly define synchronization protocols, but the implementation relies on hardware-constrained synchronization frameworks to meet the 100 ms latency threshold for critical operations.
  "
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
    The Digital Twin system follows an **entity-centric architecture** derived from **ISO 23247 RA**, structured into three primary entities:
    1. **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts **device-specific resources** (e.g., sensors, actuators) and general automotive functionalities like **Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking**, deployed on dedicated hardware.
    2. **Digital Twin Entity**: Manages **data pre-processing, actuation, and control mechanisms**, abstracting physical twin operations for higher-level coordination.
    3. **User Entity**: Contains the **singular User Interface FE**, split into **3D Visualization** and **Control Panel**, executed via ROS2 packages. Each FE is implemented as an independent ROS2 node, ensuring modularity and alignment with ISO 23247’s entity-based design.
    **Architecture Patterns**: The system leverages **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2)** for inter-entity communication, enabling distributed execution across hardware components. The **deviation from ISO 23247** lies in the **Physical Twin Entity’s broader scope** (e.g., hosting compute-agnostic functions) and the **lack of a dedicated OME (Operational Management Entity)**. No explicit orchestration layer or advanced coordination mechanisms (e.g., consensus protocols) are described.
  "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The ISO 23247 standard does not explicitly define **fidelity requirements**, **validation/verification (V&V) methods**, or **uncertainty quantification** for Digital Twin models or data. However, the framework implies:
- **Model fidelity** is achieved through **Functional Entities (FEs)** that implement domain-specific logic (e.g., process simulations) with unspecified precision thresholds.
- **Validation/Verification (V&V)** is implicitly supported by the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**, which ensures real-time synchronization between physical OMEs and their digital twins via **OPC UA** for data consistency.
- **Error detection/correction** mechanisms are likely embedded in the **User Entity’s interfaces** (e.g., alerts for discrepancies between physical and digital states).
- **Quality assurance (QA)** processes are not standardized but may rely on **ISO 14258 (OPC UA)** compliance for data integrity and **ISO 23247 Part 4 (Information Exchange)** for interoperability audits.

No specific metrics (e.g., error budgets, confidence intervals) or methodologies (e.g., Monte Carlo simulations) are mentioned, but the standard’s modular architecture suggests iterative validation via **Functional Entities (FEs)**.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for the Digital Twin System (DTS) comprises:
- **Internal Communication**:
  - **ROS2 network** (ROS 2 Distributed) for inter-SW communication within the vehicle’s onboard computing platform.
  - **No explicit security protocols** are mentioned for internal communication.

- **Inter-Entity Communication**:
  - **Wi-Fi UDP** for data transmission between the vehicle and DT entity.
  - **No mesh topology** or redundancy mechanisms are specified for Wi-Fi communication.
  - **Network Latency Constraint**: The system must maintain **≤100ms latency** from the vehicle to the DT entity (as per unspecified requirement R3).

- **Bandwidth/Latency Requirements**:
  - No explicit bandwidth or frequency requirements (e.g., 100-400 Hz) are provided; only the **100ms latency constraint** is mentioned.
  - **No redundancy or failover mechanisms** are described for network reliability.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **deployment infrastructure** for the Digital Twin System (DTS) is implemented on:
- **Onboard Computing Platform**: **NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano** microcomputer, hosting all **ROS2 packages** for each Feature Engine (FE).
- **Data Storage**:
  - **External to the vehicle** (as per Requirement R3), though the exact storage device (e.g., cloud, edge server) is not specified in the documents.
  - **No explicit computational/storage requirements** (e.g., CPU/RAM, 1TB SSD) are mentioned; only the **onboard Jetson Orin Nano** is referenced.
- **Scalability**:
  - No scalability mechanisms (e.g., load balancing, horizontal scaling) are described.
- **Deployment Models**:
  - The system appears to follow a **modular, FE-based deployment** (each FE as an individual ROS2 package).
- **Availability/Reliability**:
  - No uptime guarantees (e.g., >99.9%) or redundancy assurances are provided.
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "
    Not in Document
  "
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 – Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing** (2021), structured around an **entity-based reference architecture (RA)**. The framework defines four core entities:
  - **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**: Represents physical assets (e.g., machines, facilities, products) with observable presence or operation in manufacturing.
  - **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**: Manages data acquisition from OMEs and controls their physical operations via standardized protocols (e.g., **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** is mentioned as a viable implementation option, though not mandated).
  - **Digital Twin Entity**: Provides a digital replica of the OME, enabling simulation, monitoring, and predictive analytics.
  - **User Entity**: Facilitates human interaction through interfaces for monitoring (e.g., real-time data visualization) and remote management (e.g., OME control adjustments).

  The standard does not prescribe specific methodologies for **Functional Entities (FEs)**—smaller modular components handling tasks like data transformation or user interfacing—nor does it detail lifecycle governance or versioning strategies. The provided demonstration (e.g., [GitHub repository](https://github.com/McSCert/Automotive-Digital-Twin)) implies practical deployment but lacks explicit documentation on lifecycle phases or evolution mechanisms."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin System (DTS)** for an **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC)** system must adhere to **ISO 15622:2018**, which specifies performance requirements and test procedures for ACC, including real-time data synchronization and validation. Specifically, the system must implement ACC as defined in ISO 15622, ensuring compliance with vehicle state monitoring and control protocols.

For **security and safety considerations**, the DTS must incorporate **latency-sensitive communication constraints** as outlined in **3GPP TS 22.185 (Release 15)**, which mandates a **network latency of no more than 100 milliseconds** for V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services to ensure real-time data exchange between vehicles and infrastructure. This requirement is critical for maintaining safe operational distances and collision avoidance in adaptive cruise control applications.

While the provided documents do not explicitly reference **ISO 26262-1:2018 (Road vehicles – Functional safety)**, the underlying context implies that the DTS must incorporate **fail-safe mechanisms** and **safety-critical system considerations** to mitigate risks associated with autonomous driving functions. These could include redundant control pathways, failover protocols, and validation checks to ensure system reliability under adverse conditions (e.g., sensor failures, communication disruptions). However, no specific compliance documentation for ISO 26262 is cited in the provided sources."
]
